question.label: Self-read opener
question.name: Before I share my view, how do you think the last stretch has gone?
question.description: Anchors the conversation in their self-assessment first; gap vs manager's view becomes a signal.

answer: I think I am shipping, but I am not sure I am aiming at the highest-leverage work yet.
note: He can see a specific leverage problem in his current work, which is strong negative clarity but mild positive engagement because he is still shipping and self-assessing honestly.

question.label: Highest-leverage work
question.name: When you say 'highest-leverage work,' what work are you pointing to specifically?
question.description: Pins down the concrete work he believes would count as higher leverage before moving deeper into evidence.

answer: I can describe Principal impact in general, but this team's bar still feels fuzzy.
note: He cannot name team-specific Principal-level work and says the bar feels fuzzy, which is a strong negative clarity signal.

question.label: Team bar fuzzy
question.name: This team's bar feels fuzzy — where does that show up most concretely?
question.description: Drills into the specific place expectation fuzziness is showing up before moving on.

answer: I have mostly stayed in my lane instead of forcing cross-team decisions.
note: He names a concrete clarity failure: staying in his lane rather than stepping into cross-team decisions, which strongly signals unclear Principal expectations.

question.label: What they're proud of
question.name: What's something from the last quarter you're genuinely proud of — not what looks good, what actually was good?
question.description: Separates real wins from performative ones; calibrates their sense of impact.

answer: I have made a few calls independently, but I still over-index on being safe.
note: He names some independent judgment, but 'over-index on being safe' signals cautious, lower-leverage execution rather than strong ownership.

question.label: Thread follow
question.name: have made calls — can you say more about what that means for you right now?
question.description: Runtime thread-follow injected because planner did not follow substantive answer.

answer: I probably should ask for sharper feedback earlier than I do.
note: He names a specific missing behavior — asking for sharper feedback earlier — which signals mild under-engagement and missed growth input; also reinforces an expectation-alignment thread.

question.label: Sharper feedback
question.name: Sharper feedback earlier — what has stopped you doing that sooner?
question.description: Drills into the barrier behind delaying sharper feedback while staying on the live thread.

answer: I have not built enough trust with adjacent teams yet.
note: Says delayed feedback-seeking is due to insufficient trust with adjacent teams, which mildly signals a growth barrier; wellbeing is not engaged by the answer. [THREAD-DEFERRED] 'trust with adjacent teams' could be picked up next session, but drill cap requires advancing the arc.

question.label: Gap awareness
question.name: Where do you think you've fallen short of where you want to be?
question.description: Tests whether they can name their own gaps before the manager does.

answer: The gap is less technical and more around visible ownership.
note: He names a specific non-technical gap in visible ownership, showing clear self-awareness of the performance issue.

question.label: Questioning pattern
question.name: What keeps you from asking more questions earlier?
question.description: Surfaces the barrier behind the low-question pattern without assuming the cause.

answer: I need explicit expectations for the next cycle so I can calibrate fast.
note: Names missing explicit expectations as the barrier to calibrating early — a mild clarity and engagement deficit rather than a wellbeing issue.

question.label: Earlier signals
question.name: What would help you spot expectation drift earlier next time?
question.description: Moves toward a practical change in how he reads and surfaces alignment issues.

answer: I will pick one recent deliverable and write down the decision rule I used before our next check-in.
note: He named a concrete next-step habit to surface his decision logic earlier, which is a mild positive signal on clarity and growth; the proactive, bounded plan also suggests stable enough capacity to act.
